product_name: Build a notification prioritization system using ML

recommendation.reasoning: The decision to not pursue the notification prioritization system using ML is primarily driven by significant legal and compliance risks, including missing consent mechanisms for data collection and potential retention policy violations [risk-002, risk-003]. Additionally, the absence of a data handling justification and unmitigated high risks exceeding acceptable limits further solidifies the recommendation against proceeding with this product [risk-001]. These factors present substantial barriers that could lead to regulatory penalties and damage to the company's reputation.

executive_summary:
The proposed product is a notification prioritization system utilizing machine learning (ML) to enhance user engagement by delivering timely and relevant notifications. This initiative aims to address the growing challenge of notification overload, which has been identified as a significant pain point for users, as indicated by finding [customer-001]. However, the analysis has revealed multiple critical risks that hinder the project's viability, including the absence of a consent mechanism for data collection [risk-002] and a lack of justification for data handling practices [risk-001].

The analysis of the findings has uncovered several top risks and opportunities associated with this product. Key risks include three unmitigated high risks that exceed the acceptable threshold, specifically the missing consent mechanism [risk-002], the absence of data handling justification [risk-001], and a violation of retention policies [risk-003]. Additionally, there are high-severity requirements that need to be addressed, such as implementing the ML model for prioritization [requirements-002] and defining notification prioritization criteria [requirements-001]. The presence of these risks, coupled with the legal implications surrounding PII and privacy, has led to a risk gate failure for the project.

Based on the comprehensive analysis, the recommendation is to not pursue the development of the notification prioritization system at this time, with a confidence level of 0.85. The immediate next steps for the team include addressing the identified blockers by conducting a thorough legal review to establish compliance with PII and privacy regulations, as well as developing a clear data handling justification and consent mechanism. Additionally, the team should focus on gathering the necessary data and documentation to support any future reconsideration of this initiative.

key_findings.title: Implement Machine Learning Model for Prioritization
key_findings.description: Develop and integrate a machine learning model that can analyze user behavior and prioritize notifications accordingly.
recommendations: Gather historical user interaction data for model training.
assumptions: The model will require historical data to train effectively.

key_findings.title: Missing Consent Mechanism for Data Collection
key_findings.description: There is no evidence of a consent mechanism for data collection, which violates privacy policies.
recommendations: Develop and implement a clear consent mechanism for data collection.
assumptions: A consent mechanism is required for data collection.

key_findings.title: Define Notification Prioritization Criteria
key_findings.description: Establish clear criteria for how notifications will be prioritized using machine learning algorithms.
recommendations: Conduct user interviews to gather insights on notification preferences.
assumptions: The prioritization criteria will be based on user engagement and context.

key_findings.title: Monitoring and Alerting for Notification Performance
key_findings.description: Set up monitoring and alerting systems to track the performance of the notification prioritization system.
recommendations: Select appropriate monitoring tools for system performance.
assumptions: Monitoring tools will be integrated with the notification system.

key_findings.title: Missing Data Handling Justification
key_findings.description: The system lacks justification for data collection, which may lead to non-compliance with data handling policies.
recommendations: Implement a mechanism to justify data collection for all user data.
assumptions: Data collection justifications are required as per policy.

key_findings.title: Retention Policy Violation
key_findings.description: The system does not have a defined retention policy for user data, which may lead to data retention beyond the allowed period.
recommendations: Establish and enforce a data retention policy that limits data storage to 90 days.
assumptions: Data retention must be limited to 90 days.

key_findings.title: Accessibility Compliance Gaps
key_findings.description: The system may not fully comply with WCAG AA standards, which could hinder accessibility for users with disabilities.
recommendations: Conduct an accessibility audit to ensure compliance with WCAG AA standards.
assumptions: Accessibility is a requirement, and compliance with WCAG AA is necessary.
